key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $1.29, up from $1.13 YoY
Operating income of $264.3 million, up 7.0% YoY
Dividends per share increased to $0.50 from $0.39 YoY
qoq_changes: Capitalized internal-use software and system costs increased to $3,003.3 million from $2,817.5 million at year-end 2024
Total current assets increased to $1,463.1 million from $1,360.6 million at year-end 2024
new_risks: Macroeconomic volatility including changes in interest rates and inflation impacting consumer spending, home prices, and consumer debt
Execution and operational risks tied to the ongoing cloud technology transformation and data security infrastructure upgrades
management_tone: Cautiously optimistic, highlighting steady revenue and earnings growth while acknowledging macroeconomic headwinds and prioritizing cloud technology transformation.
analyst_note: Equifax delivered a solid Q3 2025 with revenue growing 7.1% and net income up 13.4% YoY, supported by margin expansion and a notable dividend increase. The continued capitalization of internal-use software underscores their commitment to completing their cloud transition, which should drive long-term operating leverage despite near-term macro uncertainties.
